name: Bound The 128x128 Two-Stage Feed Cadence

summary.name: Bound The 128x128 Two-Stage Feed Cadence
summary.hypothesis: The paired PTX export helper is now closed-negative, but the 128x128 two-stage sibling is still the best distinct family from the improved baseline. A bounded adjustment to its cp.async cadence, wait-group timing, or shared-memory handoff may trim the new long-scoreboard cost without bringing back the DRAM or barrier inflation that hurt the closed variants.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Feed latency and stage handoff inside the 128x128 two-stage kernel, with the long-scoreboard rise indicating that the current baseline is still waiting too long on data readiness.
summary.risk: Medium. This family already proved it can move the profile in the right direction, but the remaining gap may be a hard local limit rather than a scheduling issue.
summary.stop_condition: Keep this direction only if the next run beats 25.64300728 ms or at minimum pulls long-scoreboard stalls down without reintroducing memory or barrier pressure.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
